sections.paragraph:
unused in Kal; prob. i.q. [“לָהַג”] לָהָה pr. to burn with thirst; and, as this is the case with rabid dogs, to be rabid, mad, like a dog; to be mad. I thus understand-

Hithpalpel, part. מִתְלַהְלֵהַּ mad, insane, Pro 26:18. LXX. in the Aldine edition, and Symm. πειρώμενοι, tempted, driven (by a demon). Venet. ἐξεστώς. There is in Syriac a secondary root derived from the idea of madness, ܐܶܬܠܰܗܠܰܗ to be frightened, scared.

I.
